title: Fees and Costs Chargeable to Workers
content: Under the 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs, the placement fee chargeable to the worker shall not exceed the equivalent of one month's salary or the amount prescribed by the POEA, whichever is lower. Documentations costs, including but not limited to processing of passports, visas, and other necessary documents, shall be borne by the worker but must be reasonable and supported by official receipts. The recruitment agency is prohibited from charging any other fees or costs to the worker beyond those allowed by the POEA rules.

metadata.source_file: 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs Primer.pdf
